mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It uses custom resources to automate configuration, making it easier to manage Prometheus and Alertmanager instances. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the kube-prometheus-stack chart.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the kube-prometheus-stack
steps.description:
This command installs the kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart, which includes Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.

```bash

helm install prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.75.0 --namespace monitoring --create-namespace

```

> Change the version as needed.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Prometheus and Alertmanager pods are running in the monitoring namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Look for pods with names starting with 'prometheus-' and 'alertmanager-'

steps.title: Access the Grafana dashboard
steps.description:
This step retrieves the Grafana admin password and provides the URL to access the dashboard.

```bash

kubectl get secret prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.data.admin-password}' | base64 --decode

kubectl port-forward svc/prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring 3000:80

```

> This command retrieves the Grafana admin password.
> Access Grafana at http://localhost:3000
commands: kubectl get secret prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.data.admin-password}' | base64 --decode

uninstall.title: Uninstall the kube-prometheus-stack
uninstall.description:
This command removes the kube-prometheus-stack and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> This will remove the Helm release.
> This will delete the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the kube-prometheus-stack
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade the Helm release to the specified version.

```bash

helm upgrade prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.76.0 --namespace monitoring

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Change the version as needed.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) for Prometheus are not installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard shows 'Data source not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the Prometheus data source is not configured correctly in Grafana.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana configuration and ensure that the Prometheus service is correctly set as a data source.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration or resource limits being exceeded.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Prometheus pod using 'kubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name> -n monitoring' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Alertmanager is not sending alerts.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to misconfigured alert rules or notification channels.

**Fix:**
Verify the alert rules and notification settings in the Alertmanager configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the new alerting rules and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus and Alertmanager pods running, and you can access Grafana at http://localhost:3000 with the retrieved admin password.
codeSnippets: kubectl get secret prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.data.admin-password}' | base64 --decode

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
